I keep putting the design project on hold, and the lack of sleep just makes me feel even more isolated.

raw_response: {"simulated_user_message":"I lie awake, scrolling through old photos of my mom, thinking about how my husband says my pain is \"all in my head\" and I feel like I'm drowning in grief and an impossible deadline. The project feels like a mountain I can't climb because every time I try to start, my mind flips back to those memories and the silence in the house. I tried to focus by sketching quick thumbnails, but it just turns into a frantic doodle that never feels finished. I think the biggest hurdle is that I keep putting the project off because my body feels exhausted and my mind is stuck in a loop of guilt and sadness.","problems_referred":["sleep_problems","loneliness","procrastination","life_transition"]}

parsed.simulated_user_message: I lie awake, scrolling through old photos of my mom, thinking about how my husband says my pain is "all in my head" and I feel like I'm drowning in grief and an impossible deadline. The project feels like a mountain I can't climb because every time I try to start, my mind flips back to those memories and the silence in the house. I tried to focus by sketching quick thumbnails, but it just turns into a frantic doodle that never feels finished. I think the biggest hurdle is that I keep putting the project off because my body feels exhausted and my mind is stuck in a loop of guilt and sadness.
